mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indicates expanding manufacturing activity and aggregate supply, which directly boosts corporate earnings expectations and leads to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The average 20-day forward return following periods of INDPRO increase is not statistically distinguishable from zero at the 5% significance level over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate by more than 0.5 percentage points over three months signals the onset of labor market deterioration, reducing aggregate demand and corporate profit expectations, thus predicting negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE increases by more than 0.5 percentage points over a trailing 3-month period
failure_condition: After trigger activation, the median 20-day forward S&P 500 return is non-negative or the proportion of negative returns is not significantly greater than 50% using a binomial test at the 5% level.

mechanism_narrative: When CPI inflation exceeds 3% and the federal funds rate is below 2%, real interest rates are deeply negative, providing an extremely accommodative monetary environment that artificially supports equity valuations and fuels risk-taking, leading to positive forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: When the trigger condition is met, the average 20-day forward S&P 500 return is not positive and significant (one-sided t-test p > 0.10) over the full sample.

mechanism_narrative: During the early phase of a post-recession recovery, increases in housing starts signal renewed consumer demand and construction activity, which drives economic momentum and lifts corporate revenues, resulting in positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
regime_hint: post-recession recovery (defined as within 12 months after NBER recession trough)
failure_condition: In post-recession periods, the correlation between HOUST changes and 20-day forward SPX returns is not positive (i.e., Spearman rho <= 0) or the average return after HOUST increases is not greater than after decreases.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in nonfarm payrolls exceeding 200,000 surprises markets positively, reinforcing expectations of robust employment and consumer spending, which boosts equity prices within a very short horizon of 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS month-over-month change > 200 on release date
failure_condition: The average 5-day forward return following trigger events is not positive or the hit rate (positive returns) is below 55% over the available history.

mechanism_narrative: When both retail sales and industrial production are simultaneously above their respective 6-month moving averages, it signals a synchronized expansion in demand and supply, reducing inventory imbalances and leading to sustained positive earnings revisions that push S&P 500 higher over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS > its 6-month MA and INDPRO > its 6-month MA
failure_condition: When the joint condition holds, the median 20-day forward return is not greater than the unconditional median, or the Sharpe ratio of the signal is less than 0.2 annually.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained real GDP growth, as measured by quarterly GDP, reflects expanding aggregate output and corporate earnings capacity, which gradually lifts equity valuations over a quarter (60 trading days) in a persistent trend-following manner.
failure_condition: The rolling 3-year correlation between GDPC1 quarterly growth and subsequent 60-day SPX return is not positive (i.e., <= 0) or the strategy of being long SPX after positive GDP growth yields negative cumulative returns over any 10-year window.

mechanism_narrative: During an environment of high inflation (CPI year-over-year above 4%), any increase in the federal funds rate is perceived as a necessary but contractionary policy move that depresses future economic activity and corporate profits, resulting in negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS month-over-month increase (i.e., current value > previous month)
failure_condition: In periods where CPIAUCSL > 4% and FEDFUNDS increases month-on-month, the average 20-day forward SPX return is not negative or the proportion of negative returns is not significantly greater than 50%.
